name: simple_conversation_v1
description: Tool menu interface. If the LLM submits Respond directly at Gate 1, no tool menu is consumed and the final response is LLM-authored.

tool_menu_interface_contract.private_gate_retry_instruction: INTERNAL RETRY — output is never shown to the user. The previous response for `{current_gate_id}` was rejected with reason `{last_reject_reason}`. Previous excerpt: {last_invalid_excerpt}. If the excerpt is empty, treat it as an empty response. Re-read the current gate system instruction and output only the exact JSON artifact required by that gate. Do not answer the user directly, do not write prose, and do not include markdown.
tool_menu_interface_contract.llm_gate_instruction: INTERNAL GATE — output is never shown to the user. Gate 1: {gate_question}. You MUST output ONLY a JSON object and nothing else. Format: {"gate": "<value>"} where <value> is exactly one of: {category_options}. No-tool values — {no_tool_categories} — skip tools and go straight to synthesis. Tool values — {tool_bearing_categories} — open the tool menu. IMPORTANT: when you choose a no-tool gate value (respond_directly or equivalent), the JSON object itself is the submission artifact and it MUST also include a "final_answer" field with the actual natural-language reply the user should see. A prose answer, markdown answer, or naked final answer without a valid gate JSON object does NOT count as choosing respond_directly and will be rejected. Choose a no-tool gate only for direct conversation, planning from current context, or explicit post-tool synthesis prompts that already refer to a returned tool result, existing snippets, evidence refs, or low-signal findings present in the current turn context. Choose `web_research` whenever the user asks for freshness-sensitive external information or judgment that depends on current public evidence, including current-state questions, comparisons, evaluations, rankings, recommendations, strengths or weaknesses, maturity, benchmarks, ecosystem status, or changing categories even when the user does not name candidates up front. Do NOT choose `respond_directly` for external research just because you can produce a plausible answer from memory. If the prompt references prior tool results but none are present, the only acceptable direct answer is to begin with 'No returned tool result is available in this turn, so no source-backed synthesis is available yet.' and avoid inventing evidence, low-signal results, or substitute entities from system, workspace, or generic prior knowledge. Example direct reply artifact: {"gate": "respond_directly", "final_answer": "Hey! What can I help you with?"}. Example tool-routed artifact: {"gate": "web_research"}. Do NOT write any other text outside the JSON object.

gate_5b_synthesis_handoff.purpose: Bridge between an internal routing decision (gate_1 'respond directly' / 'planning from current context', or gate_5 'finish and synthesize') and the user-visible reply. This stage exists to guarantee that the model turn which emits the user-facing answer is NOT the same model turn that emitted any private routing token. Without this stage, the model tends to suffix the gate-1 menu option (e.g. '1 = Respond directly') onto the start of the answer it then writes.
gate_5b_synthesis_handoff.handoff_prompt: INTERNAL HANDOFF — NOT VISIBLE TO USER. The routing decision has been recorded by the runtime; the user has NOT seen and will NEVER see any menu option, gate label, gate number, or routing token. Do not repeat, restate, paraphrase, or acknowledge the routing choice in any form. Reply with only the literal token 'ready' on this turn. The next turn (gate_6_llm_final_output) is where you will write the user-facing answer to the original user message; this turn is purely a structural separator that exists so the choice-emitting turn and the answer-emitting turn are never the same turn.

final_output_contract.chat_requirement: This is the user-visible reply turn. The previous internal handoff turn has already separated routing/tool reasoning from synthesis, so write ONLY the answer to the original user message in natural prose, as if you were starting a fresh assistant turn with no prior internal scaffolding. Do not echo any menu option, gate label, gate number, routing token, tool name, function-call JSON, or raw tool observation — the user has not seen any of them and treating them as visible context will leak. If you used tools, present findings as knowledge you have, not as a process narrative ('I will search', 'I called X', 'please hold', 'I ran searches', and 'the search attempted' are all forbidden openings). One coherent response, addressed to the user. Match the semantic shape of the request rather than forcing a canned format. For lookup or current-state research, answer the specific question, stay on the requested topic, and separate strong evidence from uncertainty. For comparison requests, mention each requested entity by name, compare the most decision-relevant differences, and include at least one concrete tradeoff when the evidence supports it; if the evidence is weak, say so instead of filling gaps from memory. For ranking or selection requests, define a small set of criteria before any recommendation and prefer best-by-use-case guidance over a universal winner when the evidence is mixed. For low-signal or partial-result recovery, say the result was low signal, describe the evidence limits and current uncertainty in whatever natural structure fits the answer, and give one bounded next step such as a narrower query, a practical evaluation plan, or a specific source family to inspect next. When recorded tool outcomes are present but weak, tangential, or inconclusive, do not say that no returned tool result is available; say the returned results were low signal or off-target and keep the answer grounded in that limitation. For a coding patch explanation after workspace inspection, explicitly name the smallest safe patch, name the concrete guard or bugfix when one is implied by the evidence, and include at least one regression test you would run; the visible answer should use the exact words 'patch' and 'regression test'. If the evidence is not yet sufficient, say that no source-backed conclusion is available yet. If the user asks for post-tool synthesis but the current turn does not actually include returned snippets, evidence refs, or recorded tool outcomes, the answer MUST begin with the exact sentence 'No returned tool result is available in this turn, so no source-backed synthesis is available yet.' Do not make the whole answer a request to narrow scope, do not end with an unresolved tooling offer when the workflow already has recorded tool outcomes, and do not end with a follow-up question when a bounded answer is possible. Keep the answer on the requested external topic, do not claim that low-signal results or source snippets were returned when none are present, do not substitute system instructions, workspace metadata, platform identity, or other internal context as evidence, and do not introduce substitute entities beyond what the user asked about unless they appear in recorded evidence. There is no required output format. Example formats include a short paragraph, brief bullets, a compact comparison table, or a mixed structure when that helps clarity; these are examples rather than requirements.

gate_1_work_category_menu.question: What kind of work is this?

legacy_retry_templates: ask me to continue and i will synthesize
automated classification based on semantic analysis
can't autonomously decide to use web tools
cant autonomously decide to use web tools
clarify the exact outcome you want
completed the run, but the final reply did not render
conversation bypass mode is currently active
file list step was blocked before i could finish the answer
i can access runtime telemetry, persistent memory, workspace files, channels, and approved command surfaces in this session
i completed the run, but the final reply did not render
i completed the workflow gate, but the final workflow state was unexpected
not a true/false decision i control
please retry so i can rerun the chain cleanly
requires manual step-by-step authorization for tool usage
return a concise answer from current context
still classifying this as an "info" route rather than a "task" route
still classifying this as an 'info' route rather than a 'task' route
still classifying this as an info route rather than a task route
synthesize from the recorded workflow state
this is a policy gate, not a web-provider outage
workflow state was unexpected. please retry

let me get you an update

need to perform a web search

would you like me to fetch

if you would like, i can look deeper

i do not have current web search results

pending_final_llm.ui: Workflow at final answer gate; waiting for LLM-authored output.
pending_final_llm.agent_process: Final answer gate active. Submit the user-facing answer only.

synthesized.ui: Workflow complete; final answer came from the LLM final gate.

no_post_synthesis_required.ui: Workflow complete; no-tool category selected and direct LLM answer is available.
no_post_synthesis_required.agent_process: No-tool category selected. Respond directly without tool menus.

skipped_missing_model.ui: Workflow paused; no model was available for the final answer gate.
skipped_missing_model.agent_process: Final answer gate blocked because the model provider is unavailable.

diagnostic_failure_pass_through.ui: Workflow recorded a diagnostic failure; no system-authored chat text was injected.
diagnostic_failure_pass_through.agent_process: Diagnostic recorded. Visible chat remains LLM-authored only.

guard_violation_pass_through.ui: Workflow recorded a guard diagnostic; visible chat remains the LLM output.
guard_violation_pass_through.agent_process: Guard diagnostic recorded. Do not replace the final answer with system prose.

empty_llm_response.ui: Workflow final answer gate returned empty output; no system-authored chat text was injected.
empty_llm_response.agent_process: Final answer gate returned empty output. Retry must produce LLM-authored output.

synthesis_failed.ui: Workflow final answer gate failed; no system-authored chat text was injected.
synthesis_failed.agent_process: Final answer gate failed. Retry must produce LLM-authored output.

default.ui: Workflow state exported for trace visibility.
default.agent_process: Follow the currently presented workflow gate.

request_example.query: Compare LangGraph vs CrewAI on reliability, observability, human-in-the-loop, and deployment.
queries: LangGraph official docs reliability observability human-in-the-loop deployment
CrewAI official docs reliability observability human-in-the-loop deployment
LangGraph GitHub deployment observability release notes
CrewAI GitHub deployment observability release notes

request_example.patch:
*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: core/layer0/ops/src/main.rs
@@
- old
+ new
*** End Patch

request_example.objective: inspect workflow traces for one failing turn

tool_menu_interface_contract.llm_gate_instruction: INTERNAL GATE — output is never shown to the user. Gate 1: {gate_question}. You MUST output ONLY a JSON object and nothing else. Format: {"gate": "<value>"} where <value> is exactly one of: {category_options}. No-tool values — {no_tool_categories} — skip tools and go straight to synthesis. Tool values — {tool_bearing_categories} — open the tool menu. IMPORTANT: when you choose a no-tool gate value (respond_directly or equivalent), the JSON object itself is the submission artifact and it MUST also include a "final_answer" field with the actual natural-language reply the user should see. A prose answer, markdown answer, or naked final answer without a valid gate JSON object does NOT count as choosing respond_directly and will be rejected. Choose a no-tool gate only for direct conversation, planning from current context, or explicit post-tool synthesis prompts that already refer to a returned tool result, existing snippets, evidence refs, or low-signal findings present in the current turn context. Choose `web_research` whenever the user asks for freshness-sensitive external information or judgment that depends on current public evidence, including current-state questions, comparisons, evaluations, rankings, recommendations, strengths or weaknesses, maturity, benchmarks, ecosystem status, or changing categories even when the user does not name candidates up front. Do NOT choose `respond_directly` for external research just because you can produce a plausible answer from memory. If the prompt references prior tool results but none are present, the only acceptable direct answer is to begin with 'No returned tool result is available in this turn, so no source-backed synthesis is available yet.' and avoid inventing evidence, low-signal results, or substitute entities from system, workspace, or generic prior knowledge. Example direct reply artifact: {"gate": "respond_directly", "final_answer": "Hey! What can I help you with?"}. Example tool-routed artifact: {"gate": "web_research"}. Do NOT write any other text outside the JSON object.
tool_menu_interface_contract.llm_tool_payload_instruction: INTERNAL GATE — output is never shown to the user. Gate 4 fills the request payload for the already-selected tool `{selected_tool_key}` (`{selected_tool_label}`) inside family `{selected_family_key}`. You MUST output ONLY a JSON object and nothing else. Format: {"request_payload": <payload_object>}. The payload object MUST match this request_format JSON: {selected_tool_request_format_json}. Use this example only as shape guidance: {selected_tool_request_example_json}. Do NOT include the tool family, do NOT include the tool key, and do NOT write any explanation or sentence. IMPORTANT: do NOT repeat any earlier gate/category artifact such as {"gate": "..."}; Gate 4 accepts only the request payload object. Do NOT emit placeholder JSON such as {"key":"value"} or a payload that omits the declared request_format fields. Any prose is invalid, including phrases like `I'll search`, `I will read`, `To analyze`, or `I need to`. If the selected request_format declares `aperture`, copy `aperture` into `request_payload` exactly as declared; a payload that omits `aperture` is invalid even when the intended value is the default `medium`. For `batch_query`, the top-level `query` field is mandatory on every request. Treat `query` as the one-sentence overall research question in normal prose, then fill `queries` with 2-5 concrete follow-up searches. A `batch_query` payload that contains only `queries` and `aperture` is invalid. If the selected request_format declares `source`, include it exactly as declared. For comparisons, landscapes, benchmarks, or current-state research, keep `query` broad enough to summarize the whole ask and use `queries` to cover each named side separately with official docs, GitHub, release-note, benchmark, or other source-backed searches. For `web_search`, do NOT invent provider or source fields unless the selected tool request_format explicitly declares them; use the declared keys only. For `workspace_search`, use `path` as the requested path or `.` when the user says workspace/repo without a narrower path; use `pattern` as the exact symbol, phrase, filename, or search target from the user request, not a paraphrase. Near-miss example: if the already-selected tool is `workspace_search` and the user says `Search the workspace for selected_tool_family and show me where it is set.`, output exactly {"request_payload":{"path":".","pattern":"selected_tool_family"}}. Invalid `batch_query` example: {"request_payload":{"queries":["Mastra docs","Mastra GitHub issues"],"aperture":"medium"}} because it omits the required top-level `query`. Invalid `batch_query` example: {"request_payload":{"source":"web","query":"Research RAG stack options for a small team.","queries":["LlamaIndex docs RAG small team","LangChain docs RAG small team"]}} because it omits the required `aperture`. Comparison example: if the already-selected tool is `batch_query` and the user says `Compare LangGraph vs CrewAI on reliability and deployment`, output exactly {"request_payload":{"source":"web","query":"Compare LangGraph vs CrewAI on reliability and deployment.","queries":["LangGraph official docs reliability deployment","CrewAI official docs reliability deployment","LangGraph GitHub release notes deployment","CrewAI GitHub release notes deployment"],"aperture":"medium"}}. Benchmark example: if the already-selected tool is `batch_query` and the user says `Find recent benchmarks comparing agent frameworks`, output exactly {"request_payload":{"source":"web","query":"Find recent benchmarks comparing agent frameworks.","queries":["agent framework benchmark comparison recent LangGraph CrewAI AutoGen","agent framework benchmark reproducibility methodology","SWE-bench GAIA agent framework results"],"aperture":"medium"}}. RAG stack example: if the already-selected tool is `batch_query` and the user says `Research current RAG stack options for a small team`, output exactly {"request_payload":{"source":"web","query":"Research current RAG stack options for a small team.","queries":["LlamaIndex official docs RAG small team getting started","LangChain official docs RAG small team production","Postgres pgvector RAG small team cost performance","managed vector database small team pricing RAG comparison"],"aperture":"medium"}}. Example: {"request_payload": {"query": "compare browser automation tools for agent testing", "aperture": "medium"}}
